pesticide-basics.text: Which item is essential personal protective equipment when handling pesticides?
Which of the following are safe pesticide-handling practices? (select all that apply)

options.text: Read the product label before use
Wear an approved respirator when required
In a locked, ventilated cabinet away from food and feed

pesticide-basics.text: What should you do with empty pesticide containers? (select all that apply)

options.text: Dispose of them per local regulations
Burn them in an open pile

pesticide-basics.text: When should you read the product label?

options.text: Before buying, mixing, and applying the product
Only if you start to feel unwell

pesticide-basics.text: What does a label's "re-entry interval" tell you?

options.text: How long to wait before re-entering a treated area

pesticide-basics.text: Which conditions increase spray drift? (select all that apply)

options.text: Nozzle held at the correct low height

pesticide-basics.text: Pesticide is splashed into someone's eye. What is the correct first response?

options.text: Rinse with clean water for at least 15 minutes and seek medical help
Wait to see if it clears on its own
Apply more product to neutralise it

pesticide-basics.text: Good personal hygiene after handling pesticides includes: (select all that apply)

options.text: Washing hands and face before eating
Showering at the end of the day

pesticide-basics.text: Where should you mix and load to limit point-source contamination?

options.text: On an impermeable, bunded area away from water sources
In the middle of the crop

pesticide-basics.text: A pesticide application record should include: (select all that apply)
The signal word "DANGER" on a pesticide label means the product is:

options.text: Highly toxic — requires the greatest caution

pesticide-basics.text: Why is it important to calibrate a sprayer?

options.text: To apply the correct rate — not too much or too little
To make the tractor go faster
So the tank never needs cleaning

pesticide-basics.text: Which can be early signs of pesticide poisoning? (select all that apply)
A no-spray buffer zone next to a watercourse exists mainly to:
Checking tank-mix compatibility before combining products helps to:

options.text: Avoid mixtures that clog nozzles or react badly

tractor-safety.text: Before starting a tractor you should first:

options.text: Walk around it and check for hazards and bystanders

tractor-safety.text: Which are recommended safety measures? (select all that apply)

options.text: Fasten the seatbelt when ROPS is fitted
Carry extra riders on the drawbar
Keep bystanders clear of the working area

tractor-safety.text: On a steep slope you should:

options.text: Drive straight up or down rather than across where possible
Raise heavy implements as high as possible

tractor-safety.text: Which pre-operation checks are important? (select all that apply)

options.text: Guards and PTO shields in place

tractor-safety.text: What is the safest way to mount or dismount a tractor?

options.text: Maintain three points of contact, facing the machine
Jump off while it is still moving
Step down onto the rear tyre only
Climb out over the rear linkage

tractor-safety.text: Which are correct power take-off (PTO) safety rules? (select all that apply)

options.text: Keep the PTO shields in place
Wear loose clothing near the shaft
Never step over a rotating shaft

tractor-safety.text: Carrying an extra rider on a tractor is:

options.text: Not allowed unless a proper instructor/passenger seat is fitted

tractor-safety.text: If a tractor fitted with ROPS begins to roll over, you should:

options.text: Stay in the seat with the seatbelt fastened
Stand up and grip the wheel harder

tractor-safety.text: Which are correct hitching practices? (select all that apply)

options.text: Hitch towed loads only to the drawbar
Hitch high onto the axle for heavy pulls
Use the correct pins and safety chains

tractor-safety.text: Before leaving the tractor you should:

options.text: Lower implements, apply the park brake, stop the engine, and remove the key
Leave the key in for the next person

tractor-safety.text: Which apply when travelling on a public road? (select all that apply)

options.text: Use lights and a slow-moving-vehicle emblem
Travel at full speed regardless of the load

tractor-safety.text: Correct ballast and tyre pressure matter mainly because they affect:
A slow-moving-vehicle (SMV) emblem warns other road users that the vehicle:

options.text: Travels well below normal traffic speed

tractor-safety.text: To reduce run-over risk when children may be nearby: (select all that apply)

options.text: Know where everyone is before moving off
Keep children out of the work area
Let them ride in the loader bucket

tractor-safety.text: The differential lock is best used when:

options.text: One wheel loses traction, at low speed in a straight line

tractor-safety.text: Refuelling a tractor should be done:

options.text: With the engine off and away from ignition sources
With the engine running to save time
In a sealed shed with no ventilation

exam-5.text: What does a standard soil test most commonly measure?
Why is soil organic matter valuable?

options.text: It improves structure, water-holding, and nutrient supply
It makes soil heavier for no reason

exam-5.text: Which practices help build organic matter? (select all that apply)
A soil pH of 4.5 is best described as:
Which are macronutrients essential for crops? (select all that apply)
Applying nutrients based on a soil test rather than a fixed recipe helps to:

options.text: Match supply to crop need and reduce waste

exam-5.text: Which reduce nutrient loss to water? (select all that apply)

exam-6.text: In certified organic systems, synthetic nitrogen fertiliser is:
The main purpose of the organic conversion period is to:

options.text: Allow soil and system to meet organic standards before certified sale

exam-6.text: Which records are typically required for organic certification? (select all that apply)
For organic weed control a grower would prefer:
Acceptable organic soil-fertility inputs often include: (select all that apply)
Buffer zones between organic and conventional land exist to:

options.text: Prevent contamination and spray drift onto organic crops

exam-6.text: An organic inspection is usually carried out:

options.text: At least annually by an approved certifier

exam-6.text: Crop rotation in organic systems helps to: (select all that apply)

options.text: Eliminate the need for any planning

exam-6.text: GMO seed in a certified organic crop is:
Split, non-organic parallel production must be:

exam-7.text: Before harvesting, the combine header height should be set to:

options.text: Suit the crop and cut cleanly without picking up soil

exam-7.text: Pre-harvest combine checks include: (select all that apply)

options.text: Grain tank and unloading auger clear

exam-7.text: Excessive grain loss out the back of the combine often indicates:

options.text: Incorrect fan speed or concave settings

exam-7.text: Combine fires are most commonly caused by:

options.text: Chaff build-up near hot engine and bearing surfaces

exam-7.text: Safe unblocking of a plugged combine requires: (select all that apply)
Higher drum/rotor speed than needed tends to:
When moving a combine on a public road you should:

options.text: Fit lights/markers and remove or secure the header per rules

exam-7.text: Signs the concave clearance is too tight include: (select all that apply)

options.text: Perfectly clean sample with no losses
Regularly, with the machine level and engine off

exam-8.text: A good pre-season service includes: (select all that apply)

options.text: At the intervals in the operator's manual

exam-8.text: A slack drive belt will typically cause:
Before working under a raised implement you should: (select all that apply)

options.text: Stop the engine and remove the key

exam-8.text: Checking tyre pressures matters because it affects:
Old or contaminated hydraulic fluid can lead to:
Keeping a maintenance log helps to: (select all that apply)

exam-9.text: Low-stress stock handling relies mainly on:

options.text: Understanding the animal's flight zone and point of balance

exam-9.text: The 'five freedoms' of animal welfare include: (select all that apply)
A sick animal is often best managed by:

options.text: Isolating and observing it, and seeking veterinary advice
Mixing it with the whole herd

exam-9.text: Signs of ill health in livestock include: (select all that apply)
Good race and yard design should:

options.text: Use curves and solid sides to encourage smooth flow

exam-9.text: Clean, fresh water for livestock should be:
Before transporting animals you should check: (select all that apply)

options.text: The vehicle is clean and safe
The colour of the driver's hat

exam-10.text: Before attaching the cluster, teats should be:

options.text: Cleaned and, where used, fore-milked and pre-dipped

exam-10.text: Good milking hygiene includes: (select all that apply)

options.text: Skipping all cleaning to save time

exam-10.text: Rapid cooling of milk after collection is important to:

options.text: Limit bacterial growth and protect quality

exam-10.text: A high somatic cell count in milk often indicates:
Cleaning-in-place (CIP) of milking equipment typically uses: (select all that apply)
Bulk tank milk temperature should generally be held:

options.text: Chilled to the required storage temperature

exam-11.text: The main goal of pruning a fruit tree is to:

options.text: Remove as much wood as possible
Make it as tall as possible

exam-11.text: Which cuts/removals are commonly made? (select all that apply)

options.text: Strong healthy scaffold limbs at random

exam-11.text: Most pip fruit (apples/pears) are best pruned:
A pruning cut should be made:

options.text: Just above an outward-facing bud or at the branch collar
In the middle of an internode leaving a long stub

exam-11.text: Good pruning hygiene includes: (select all that apply)

options.text: Removing prunings from around the tree
Monitoring and combining methods, using chemicals as a last resort
Spraying on a fixed calendar regardless of pests

exam-12.text: Powdery mildew on grapevines typically appears as:

options.text: A white-grey powdery coating on leaves and berries

exam-12.text: Cultural controls that reduce vine disease include: (select all that apply)

options.text: Leaving the canopy as dense as possible

exam-12.text: Scouting the vineyard regularly allows you to:

options.text: Detect pests and disease early and act in time

exam-12.text: When applying an approved plant-protection product you should: (select all that apply)

options.text: Follow the label rate and interval
Spray in strong wind for wider coverage

exam-12.text: Rotating chemical mode-of-action groups helps to:
